sourceMovie.title: Supervixens
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Supervixens (1975) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Russ Meyer's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: The Nice Guys
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Supervixens clicked for you, The Nice Guys (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Supervixens?
faq.answer: Try The Nice Guys, Focus, The Voices—each shares DNA with Supervixens while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Supervixens?
faq.answer: Supervixens (1975) is best described as Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Supervixens part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Russ Meyer's Vixens Trilogy" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
